description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages: review and test. In the review stage define the jobs CleanReviewTemplate, ReviewTemplate, Clean Up Review, Clean up master review, Clean-up-master, and Deploy Review, all using the ${SHIFTIGRADE_IMAGE}:${SHIFTIGRADE_VERSION} image and a before_script that runs `oc login ${OPENSHIFT_URL} --token ${KUBE_TOKEN}` and `cd /opt/shiftigrade`; CleanReviewTemplate runs a conditional block that sets many deployment variables, deletes OpenShift resources with `oc delete bc,cm,dc,is,routes,secrets,svc -l gitlab-deployment="${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}-cloudigrade"` and the DB deployment, executes two ansible‑playbook commands to remove AWS resources and SQS, and prints a cleanup message; ReviewTemplate runs the same variable setup, then provisions AWS resources via an ansible‑playbook, ensures a PostgreSQL DB exists with `oc process ... | oc apply -f -`, applies the review template with `kontemplate`, starts builds for API and Nginx using `oc start-build -F c-${DEPLOYMENT_PREFIX}-api` and `-nginx`, rolls out three deployments, creates a Django superuser through `oc rsh ... python manage.py shell`, and prints a deployment summary; Clean Up Review is a manual job that exports DEPLOYMENT_PREFIX and DJANGO_DATABASE_HOST, deletes the same OpenShift resources, runs the same ansible cleanup playbooks, and runs only on branch pipelines; Clean up master review and Clean-up-master reuse the CleanReviewTemplate script but are triggered only on pipeline triggers and chat events respectively; Deploy Review creates a review environment named `review/${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}` with `on_stop: Clean Up Review`, sets API and AUTH route hosts, runs the same ansible provisioning, creates the DB if missing, applies the review template, starts builds, rolls out deployments, and prints the access URL. In the test stage add a Cloudigrade Docker job using the docker:stable image with a docker:dind service that pulls the latest image with `docker pull ${IMAGE_NAME}:latest || true` and builds a new image with `docker build --cache-from ${IMAGE_NAME}:latest .`, running on any branch except chat and trigger.
